# eme

> eme is a free, open-source (MIT) terminal tool that runs each AI coding agent in its own git worktree as a real tmux session, and shows — across every worktree — which agent is waiting for input, which is running, and which is idle. It is mission control for your AI coding agents.

eme is for developers who run several AI coding agents in parallel (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini, opencode, and any other command-line agent on the PATH) and need to see, at a glance, which one needs them. Each project maps to a tmux session and each git worktree to a tmux window; agents run inside your real tmux, not a hidden one a TUI owns. eme stays out of the way — there is no diff/merge/approval GUI, so review stays in your editor and in git.

## Install

- Homebrew: `brew install jinmugo/tap/eme`
- Install script: `curl -fsSL https://eme.jinmu.me/install.sh | sh`
- Go: `go install github.com/JinmuGo/eme/cmd/eme@latest`

## Key facts

- License: MIT (open source).
- Platforms: macOS and Linux.
- Requirements: tmux >= 3.0, git >= 2.30, and an AI agent on your PATH.
- Each worktree's agent shows one of: running, waiting (needs input), idle, exited, or crashed.
- The dashboard tallies how many agents need you and lets you jump straight to them.
- Bind it to a tmux key (e.g. `bind-key a display-popup -E -w 80% -h 80% eme`) to open the dashboard in a popup.

## Links

- Homepage: https://eme.jinmu.me
- Source code: https://github.com/JinmuGo/eme
- Documentation: https://github.com/JinmuGo/eme/blob/main/docs/usage.md
- Releases: https://github.com/JinmuGo/eme/releases
- Install script: https://eme.jinmu.me/install.sh
